name: Amaranth Pancakes
recipeIngredient: 1/2 tsp guar gum (might be unnecessary if Ener-G egg replacer is used)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the dry ingredients: amaranth flour, Ener-G egg replacer, and guar gum in a large bowl.
2. Gradually add water to the dry mixture, stirring until well combined. Add more water if needed to achieve a batter consistency.
3. Gently fold in the banana slices into the batter, ensuring they are evenly distributed but still visible on top of the pancakes.
4. Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
5. Pour the batter onto the hot skillet, forming small pancakes.
6. Cook until bubbles form on the surface and the edges start to look dry, about 2-3 minutes.
7. Flip the pancakes using a spatula, cooking for another 1-2 minutes on the other side, or until golden brown.
8. Serve the pancakes with banana slices facing up. The warm bananas will add sweetness to the pancakes.
9. These pancakes are fun and appealing to children.
